content:
# Galatians Chapter 3 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/GAL_003_page_001.jpg, GAL_003_page_002.jpg

1. O foolish Galatians! Who bewitched you, before whose eyes Yeshua Christos was publicly portrayed [as] crucified?

2. This only I want to learn from you: did you receive the Spirit from works of law or from hearing of faith?

3. Are you so foolish? Having begun by [the] Spirit, are you now being completed by [the] flesh?

4. Did you suffer so many things in vain — if indeed [it was] in vain?

5. The [one] therefore supplying to you the Spirit and working miracles among you — [is it] from works of law or from hearing of faith?

6. Just as Abraham believed God, and it was credited to him as righteousness.

7. Know then that the [ones] of faith — these are sons of Abraham.

8. And the Scripture, having foreseen that God would justify the nations from faith, proclaimed the gospel beforehand to Abraham: "In you all the nations will be blessed."

9. So then the [ones] of faith are blessed with the faithful Abraham.

10. For as many as are from works of law are under a curse; for it is written: "Cursed [is] everyone who does not remain in all the [things] having been written in the book of the law, to do them."

11. And that by law no one is justified before God [is] evident, because "the righteous [one] will live from faith."

12. But the law is not from faith, but "the [one] having done them will live by them."

13. Christos redeemed us from the curse of the law, having become a curse for us — because it is written: "Cursed [is] everyone hanging upon a tree" —

14. that the blessing of Abraham might come to the nations in Christos Yeshua, that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through the faith.

15. Brothers, I speak according to man: even a man's having-been-ratified covenant no one sets aside or adds to.

16. Now the promises were spoken to Abraham and to his seed. It does not say "and to seeds," as of many, but as of one: "and to your seed," who is Christos.

17. And this I say: a covenant having been ratified beforehand by God, the law, having come four hundred and thirty years later, does not annul, so as to abolish the promise.

18. For if the inheritance [is] from law, [it is] no longer from a promise; but God has graciously given [it] to Abraham through a promise.

19. Why then the law? It was added because of the transgressions, until the seed should come to whom the promise had been made, having been arranged through angels by [the] hand of a mediator.

20. Now a mediator is not of one, but God is one.

21. Is the law then against the promises of God? May it never be! For if a law had been given which was able to give life, then indeed righteousness would have been from law.

22. But the Scripture confined all [things] under sin, that the promise from faith of Yeshua Christos might be given to the [ones] believing.

23. But before the faith came, we were being guarded under law, confined until the coming faith was to be revealed.

24. So that the law has become our guardian unto Christos, that we might be justified from faith.

25. But the faith having come, we are no longer under a guardian.

26. For you are all sons of God through the faith in Christos Yeshua.

27. For as many of you as were baptized into Christos have put on Christos.

28. There is not Ioudaios nor Greek, there is not slave nor free, there is not male and female; for you are all one in Christos Yeshua.

29. And if you [are] of Christos, then you are seed of Abraham, heirs according to [the] promise.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 — "publicly portrayed as crucified":** proegraphē estaurōmenos — the verb prographō means "to write/display publicly" (as in a public notice). Paul says Christ was "displayed publicly as crucified" before the Galatians' eyes — a vivid reference to his preaching, which made the crucifixion visible as if on a public placard.

2. **v.8 — "proclaimed the gospel beforehand":** proeuēngelisato — a compound verb found only here in the NT: pro ("before") + euangelizomai ("proclaim good news"). Scripture itself "pre-gospeled" Abraham.

3. **v.13 — "hanging upon a tree":** epi xylou — Paul quotes Deuteronomy 21:23. The word xylon means "wood/tree" — not stauros (cross). The application of this Deuteronomic curse to crucifixion was a radical theological move.

4. **v.16 — "seed" singular:** sperma — Paul builds an argument on the singular form of "seed" (sperma, not spermata). This rabbinic-style argument identifies the singular "seed" of the Abrahamic promise with Christ.

5. **v.19 — "arranged through angels by the hand of a mediator":** This verse is notoriously difficult. The law was mediated — given through angels via Moses. The implication is that the promise, given directly by God to Abraham, is superior because it required no mediator.

6. **v.28 — "not male and female":** Note the shift from "neither...nor" (ouk...oude) to "not...and" (ouk...kai) for the final pair. "Not male and female" echoes Genesis 1:27 LXX ("male and female he created them") — Paul may be undoing the creation categories themselves in Christ.

— EMET, AI Translator
